title: Day 06 Quiz — Custom Hooks, Debounce, and Layout
description: Verify you understand custom hooks, debounce, responsive layout, and composition before Day 07 machine coding.

questions.prompt: What makes a function a custom hook in React?

options.text: Its name starts with use and it may call other hooks

questions.explanation: Custom hooks are functions named useSomething that compose built-in hooks. Rules of Hooks apply — only call from components or other hooks.

questions.prompt: Debounce delays invoking a function until the user stops triggering it for a specified wait period.
questions.explanation: Debounce collapses rapid events — typing in search — into one update after pause, reducing API calls.

questions.prompt: useDebounce returns the value after delay. If value changes at t=0 and delay=300ms, when does debouncedValue update?
questions.code:
// User types 'a' at t=0, 'ab' at t=100, stops typing
// useDebounce(value, 300) updates 300ms after last change
questions.explanation: Debounce timer resets on each change. Last change at t=100ms + 300ms delay = update around t=400ms.

questions.prompt: Why extract debounce logic into useDebounce instead of duplicating setTimeout in two components?

options.text: Reuse stateful logic with correct cleanup in one tested place

questions.explanation: Custom hooks share logic — including useEffect cleanup clearing timers — without copy-paste.

questions.prompt: Which belong in a useDebounce implementation? Select all that apply.

options.text: useState or useEffect to manage delayed value

questions.explanation: Debounce hook uses effect + timer + cleanup. Fetch belongs in effect or handler reacting to debounced value.

questions.prompt: Custom hooks that return a tuple should be typed explicitly, e.g. {{blank1}}Debounce<T>(value: T, delay: number): {{blank2}}
questions.explanation: Naming convention use prefix; generic return type preserves input value type.

questions.prompt: Complete the hook name for extracting theme toggle logic.
questions.explanation: Custom hooks must start with use — useTheme follows convention.

questions.prompt: Mobile sidebar should collapse into a drawer. Which CSS approach fits today's scope?

options.text: display:none on desktop only with media queries and toggle state
Remove sidebar component entirely on mobile

questions.explanation: Media queries + boolean isOpen state for mobile menu is standard responsive pattern without new libraries.

questions.prompt: When would you choose a custom hook over a plain utility function?
questions.sampleAnswer: When the shared logic needs React state, effects, or other hooks — debounce with timers, theme with localStorage, fetch with loading state. Plain functions for pure transforms without React lifecycle.

questions.prompt: Search fires on every debouncedValue change in useEffect. debouncedValue updates but results flicker. Missing cleanup on fetch effect. Fix?

options.text: AbortController or cancelled flag in useEffect cleanup

questions.explanation: Debounced search still races requests — combine debounce with abort/cleanup from Day 04.
